sentences.en: The tenant had lived there for a while(memory is fuzzy, I think it was years, but don't quote me on that) and in the end only paid for electricity.I hear you guys like suing eachother in the states, seems like this kind of regulation would suit you just fine!
sentences.zh: 房客在那里住了一段时间（记忆模糊，我想是好几年了，但不要引用我的话），最后只付了电费。听说你们在美国喜欢互相起诉，看来这种规定很适合你们！

blocks.html_en: In Europe we've used regulations. There's standardised ways to measure area of an apartment/house, and it has to be included in any ads and contract of sale/rent. If the actual area doesn't match what's in the ads/contract the landlord/seller will be in trouble. I saw a news article once where the landlord,iirc, had to return all rent money to the tenant. The tenant had lived there for a while(memory is fuzzy, I think it was years, but don't quote me on that) and in the end only paid for electricity.

sentences.en: In Europe we've used regulations.
sentences.zh: 在欧洲，我们使用法规。

sentences.en: There's standardised ways to measure area of an apartment/house, and it has to be included in any ads and contract of sale/rent.
sentences.zh: 有标准化的方法来测量公寓/房屋的面积，并且它必须包含在任何广告和销售/租赁合同中。

sentences.en: If the actual area doesn't match what's in the ads/contract the landlord/seller will be in trouble.
sentences.zh: 如果实际面积与广告/合同中的面积不符，房东/卖家就会遇到麻烦。

sentences.en: I saw a news article once where the landlord,iirc, had to return all rent money to the tenant.
sentences.zh: 我曾经看到一篇新闻文章，房东必须将所有租金退还给房客。

sentences.en: The tenant had lived there for a while(memory is fuzzy, I think it was years, but don't quote me on that) and in the end only paid for electricity.
sentences.zh: 房客在那里住了一段时间（记忆模糊，我想是好几年了，但不要引用我的话），最后只付了电费。

blocks.html_en: I hear you guys like suing eachother in the states, seems like this kind of regulation would suit you just fine!

sentences.en: I hear you guys like suing eachother in the states, seems like this kind of regulation would suit you just fine!
sentences.zh: 我听说你们在美国喜欢互相起诉，看来这种规定很适合你们！

blocks.html_en:
The parent was talking about it being a "landlords market" and wanting that to be changed. I asked how that might be possible.

Improving advertisements to make them accurate, detailed, and directly-comparable is obviously a good thing. But does not change the market in favour of the tenants; the status-quo exists because of a lack of properties. That means no matter how bad the advert(s) tenants have to choose one available. If there were a surplus of properties then it would be a tenant's market.

(I'm in Europe!)

sentences.en: The parent was talking about it being a "landlords market" and wanting that to be changed.
sentences.zh: 家长说这是一个“房东市场”，并希望改变这种情况。

sentences.en: I asked how that might be possible.Improving advertisements to make them accurate, detailed, and directly-comparable is obviously a good thing.
sentences.zh: 我问这怎么可能。改进广告，使其准确、详细、可直接比较，显然是一件好事。

sentences.en: But does not change the market in favour of the tenants; the status-quo exists because of a lack of properties.
sentences.zh: 但不会改变市场对租户有利的情况；由于缺乏财产而存在现状。

sentences.en: That means no matter how bad the advert(s) tenants have to choose one available.
sentences.zh: 这意味着无论广告有多糟糕，租户都必须选择一个可用的。

sentences.en: If there were a surplus of properties then it would be a tenant's market.(I'm in Europe!)
sentences.zh: 如果有剩余的房产，那就是租户市场。（我在欧洲！）

blocks.html_en:
Advertising is already a pretty strict domain by necessity. We used to allow just about every lie under the sun in advertising, and it was universally bad. We systematically broke that down one by one over many decades, and we're still doing it! I mean, these days you can't even sell tobacco without saying "hey we're trying to kill you btw".

This is a very well-trodden path, I think.

sentences.en: Advertising is already a pretty strict domain by necessity.
sentences.zh: 广告必然已经是一个相当严格的领域。

sentences.en: We used to allow just about every lie under the sun in advertising, and it was universally bad.
sentences.zh: 过去，我们几乎允许广告中的每一个谎言，而这通常都是不好的。

sentences.en: We systematically broke that down one by one over many decades, and we're still doing it!
sentences.zh: 几十年来，我们系统地把它一一分解，而且我们仍在这样做！

sentences.en: I mean, these days you can't even sell tobacco without saying "hey we're trying to kill you btw".This is a very well-trodden path, I think.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，如今，你甚至不能在销售烟草时不说“嘿，顺便说一句，我们正试图杀了你”。我认为，这是一条非常常见的道路。

blocks.html_en:
…and define rules for the measurement taken, like no stairs, count areas below roof sloping or outside on balconies etc. with a lower factor, exclude shared areas and cellars/attics, and so on.

Not measuring correctly, not publishing the measurements, or publishing incorrect measurements outside an error margin gets counted as deception. Offer a simple reporting contact point to citizens to report landlords or offers that violate this regulation without a lawsuit.

sentences.en: …and define rules for the measurement taken, like no stairs, count areas below roof sloping or outside on balconies etc. with a lower factor, exclude shared areas and cellars/attics, and so on.Not measuring correctly, not publishing the measurements, or publishing incorrect measurements outside an error margin gets counted as deception.
sentences.zh: …并定义测量规则，例如没有楼梯，以较低的系数计算屋顶倾斜下方或阳台外部等的区域，排除共享区域和地窖/阁楼等。不正确测量，不发布测量结果，或在误差范围之外发布不正确的测量结果将被视为欺骗。

sentences.en: Offer a simple reporting contact point to citizens to report landlords or offers that violate this regulation without a lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 为公民提供一个简单的举报联系点，以举报违反此规定的房东或优惠，而无需提起诉讼。

blocks.html_en:
During the press conference he finished with a light joke that was something like “after all it’s meant to be Street Easy not Street Hard”. I assumed that was an app, your post unintentionally closed the loop for me!

Agree AI modified listing make no sense to allow; regulation here is making up for platform failure.

blocks.html_en:
The motivation is good, the implementation sucks.

> requiring disclosure of AI-altered listings

So all the landlords will just disclose it and everyone will continue to be deceived. Just like every other picture on Meta platforms these days says it is AI-edited. That didn't make the feed any better; it's still a feed full of disclosed AI crap.

Why is this mayor so fucking chicken to just BAN AI-altered listings altogether? Have jail time and $1M fines for it. Fines big enough that you would risk losing your property altogether. We need more real leaders.

blocks.html_en:
I'm just tired of regulators realizing the right things to regulate but not implementing them correctly.

Job postings must include a salary range? Who the fuck thought of that? Did they even ask a mathematician whether providing the min() and max() would be useful rather than mean(), median(), and std(), which is what the law really should require?

blocks.html_en:
How many people who are considering hiring their first few employees have an idea of what the minimum they’d likely pay and the maximum they’d likely pay for those new positions? Almost all.

Now how many could even tell you the rough definition of standard deviation, let alone be able to give a value for it? Almost none.

As an applicant for a single opening, it’s not obvious that standard deviation is helpful either.

For normal people, I think that min and max are the two most practical and understandable measures.

blocks.html_en:
> As an applicant for a single opening

You're nitpicking at details. I'm sure you're smart enough to figure out how to write it so that this isn't an issue, but it seems you're more interested in "taking the other side" rather than thinking together about how to increase job compensation transparency.

Here's the problem with minimums: Companies posting ranges of $100K-$900K. Yes, I've seen them.

Here's a hint for everyone else: For small companies and single openings they can just aggregate mean/median/std by job level (E5 average, M1 average), or aggregate horizontally by team (HR average, ops average), whatever. Anything to increase transparency. If you're criticizing the math instead of building on the math, you're clearly against job transparency.

sentences.en: > As an applicant for a single openingYou're nitpicking at details.
sentences.zh: > 作为一个职位空缺的申请人，您对细节吹毛求疵。

sentences.en: I'm sure you're smart enough to figure out how to write it so that this isn't an issue, but it seems you're more interested in "taking the other side" rather than thinking together about how to increase job compensation transparency.Here's the problem with minimums: Companies posting ranges of $100K-$900K.
sentences.zh: 我相信你足够聪明，能够弄清楚如何编写它，这样这就不是问题了，但你似乎更感兴趣的是“站在另一边”，而不是一起思考如何提高工作薪酬透明度。这是最低限度的问题：公司发布的薪酬范围为 10 万美元至 90 万美元。

sentences.en: Yes, I've seen them.Here's a hint for everyone else: For small companies and single openings they can just aggregate mean/median/std by job level (E5 average, M1 average), or aggregate horizontally by team (HR average, ops average), whatever.
sentences.zh: 是的，我见过他们。这里给其他人一个提示：对于小公司和单一职位空缺，他们可以按工作级别（E5 平均值、M1 平均值）汇总平均值/中值/标准差，或按团队水平汇总（HR 平均值、运营平均值），等等。

sentences.en: Anything to increase transparency.
sentences.zh: 任何可以增加透明度的事情。

sentences.en: If you're criticizing the math instead of building on the math, you're clearly against job transparency.
sentences.zh: 如果你批评数学而不是建立在数学基础上，那么你显然反对工作透明度。

sentences.en: Here's a hint for everyone else: For small companies and single openings they can just aggregate mean/median/std by job level (E5 average, M1 average), or aggregate horizontally by team (HR average, ops average), whatever.
sentences.zh: 这里给其他人一个提示：对于小公司和单一职位空缺，他们可以按职位级别（E5 平均值、M1 平均值）汇总平均值/中值/标准差，或按团队水平汇总（HR 平均值、运营平均值），等等。

sentences.en: Anything to increase transparency.
sentences.zh: 任何可以增加透明度的事情。

sentences.en: If you're criticizing the math instead of building on the math, you're clearly against job transparency.
sentences.zh: 如果你批评数学而不是建立在数学基础上，那么你显然反对工作透明度。

blocks.html_en:
I’m in favor of transparency (and consistently argue for that and act accordingly inside my own company). I’m against adding information that will confuse far more people than it will help.

A company listing $100k-$900k per year for a role that’s not commissioned sales is exhibiting malicious compliance and should be dealt with directly as such rather than adding an additional nearly useless measure to somehow help smoke that out or avoid it.

sentences.en: I’m in favor of transparency (and consistently argue for that and act accordingly inside my own company).
sentences.zh: 我赞成透明度（并且一直主张这一点，并在我自己的公司内部采取相应的行动）。

sentences.en: I’m against adding information that will confuse far more people than it will help.A company listing $100k-$900k per year for a role that’s not commissioned sales is exhibiting malicious compliance and should be dealt with directly as such rather than adding an additional nearly useless measure to somehow help smoke that out or avoid it.

blocks.html_en:
> Companies posting ranges of $100K-$900K.

Yes, this is extremely Netflix. The "fix" for this is to mandate that they can no longer post an ad for engineers, but rather they have to post separate ads for each engineering role. Maybe this is better, maybe not. If a person applies to L4, are they allowed to be hired at L3 pay? Will that confuse people?

> For small companies and single openings

levels.fyi chooses not to publish some comp datapoints because there's too little data, which means publishing the data is tantamount to publishing an individual's compensation.

I hear you on the goal, but the details are tough.

blocks.html_en:
Caveat: I am pro more transparency in compensation data. But it's hard to do right.

> mean(), median(), and std()

play this out a bit, and you'll quickly realize that there are infinite ways to game this. Something like levels.fyi only works because levels are a thing, and they are consistent across a relative handful of companies (most tech workers do not work at those companies).

And even levels.fyi won't publish salary data until there are enough data points at a company to prevent such publication from inadvertently publishing how much an individual person makes. This makes it less useful for (say) a senior looking for a job at a non-tech or small firm.

sentences.en: Caveat: I am pro more transparency in compensation data.
sentences.zh: 警告：我赞成薪酬数据更加透明。

sentences.en: But it's hard to do right.> mean(), median(), and std()play this out a bit, and you'll quickly realize that there are infinite ways to game this.
sentences.zh: 但这很难做到正确。>mean()、median() 和 std() 稍微发挥一下这个作用，你很快就会意识到有无数种方法可以玩这个游戏。

sentences.en: Something like levels.fyi only works because levels are a thing, and they are consistent across a relative handful of companies (most tech workers do not work at those companies).And even levels.fyi won't publish salary data until there are enough data points at a company to prevent such publication from inadvertently publishing how much an individual person makes.
sentences.zh: 像levels.fyi这样的东西之所以有效，是因为级别是一个东西，而且它们在相对少数的公司中是一致的（大多数技术人员不在这些公司工作）。甚至levels.fyi也不会发布薪资数据，直到公司有足够的数据点来防止此类出版物无意中发布个人的收入。

sentences.en: This makes it less useful for (say) a senior looking for a job at a non-tech or small firm.
sentences.zh: 这使得它对于（比如说）在非科技公司或小公司寻找工作的资深人士来说不太有用。

blocks.html_en:
I wish there would be a similar thing for food as well. In my country (Brazil), food apps such as iFood are FULL of AI images. Worse is that iFood don't allow reviews to post pictures, so you get very blind asking it.

Now Didi came (99) and Keeta, and they at least allow users to post reviews with pictures.

sentences.en: In my country (Brazil), food apps such as iFood are FULL of AI images.

sentences.en: Worse is that iFood don't allow reviews to post pictures, so you get very blind asking it.Now Didi came (99) and Keeta, and they at least allow users to post reviews with pictures.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是iFood不允许评论发图片，所以你问起来就很盲目。现在滴滴来了（99）和Keeta，他们至少允许用户发表带图片的评论。

blocks.html_en:
It's super annoying, but this is a total nothing burger because he doesn't actually have any power to do anything here.

This has also been a problem long before AI with "virtually staged" apartments.

blocks.html_en:
The Mayor is not a dictator and can't just make up laws or regulations.

He can probably get DCWP to engage in the normal rule making process, but at most this is probably going to get some AI disclosure somewhere, which is what we had for "virtually staged" lies.

sentences.en: The Mayor is not a dictator and can't just make up laws or regulations.He can probably get DCWP to engage in the normal rule making process, but at most this is probably going to get some AI disclosure somewhere, which is what we had for "virtually staged" lies.
sentences.zh: 市长不是独裁者，不能只是制定法律或法规。他可能可以让 DCWP 参与正常的规则制定过程，但这最多可能会在某个地方得到一些人工智能披露，这就是我们“虚拟上演”的谎言。

sentences.zh: 市长不是独裁者，不能只是制定法律或法规。

blocks.html_en: He can probably get DCWP to engage in the normal rule making process, but at most this is probably going to get some AI disclosure somewhere, which is what we had for "virtually staged" lies.

sentences.en: He can probably get DCWP to engage in the normal rule making process, but at most this is probably going to get some AI disclosure somewhere, which is what we had for "virtually staged" lies.
sentences.zh: 他可能会让 DCWP 参与正常的规则制定过程，但这最多可能会在某个地方得到一些人工智能披露，这就是我们所拥有的“虚拟上演”谎言。

blocks.html_en:
Right, which is why all we're going to get it a label saying that AI was used (or maybe landlords will try to fly through on the label of "virtually staged" that they've been using).

Existing law doesn't have the authority to ban all AI images as inherently deceptive, and DCWP isn't going to be spending a bunch of time prosecuting individual images.

I agree with Mamdani that these images are often deceptive and misleading and sifting through the bullshit is annoying (and was annoying with virtually staged images too). It's just not going to go anywhere. The energy would be better spent on zoning and building code reform.

sentences.en: Right, which is why all we're going to get it a label saying that AI was used (or maybe landlords will try to fly through on the label of "virtually staged" that they've been using).Existing law doesn't have the authority to ban all AI images as inherently deceptive, and DCWP isn't going to be spending a bunch of time prosecuting individual images.I agree with Mamdani that these images are often deceptive and misleading and sifting through the bullshit is annoying (and was annoying with virtually staged images too).
sentences.zh: 是的，这就是为什么我们要给它贴上一个标签，说明使用了人工智能（或者房东可能会试图在他们一直使用的“虚拟上演”标签上跳过）。现有法律没有权力禁止所有人工智能图像，因为它们本质上具有欺骗性，而且 DCWP 不会花大量时间起诉单个图像。我同意 Mamdani 的观点，即这些图像通常具有欺骗性和误导性，并会通过筛选废话很烦人（对于虚拟的舞台图像也很烦人）。

sentences.en: It's just not going to go anywhere.
sentences.zh: 它不会去任何地方。

sentences.en: The energy would be better spent on zoning and building code reform.
sentences.zh: 最好把精力花在分区和建筑法规改革上。

blocks.html_en:
> The energy would be better spent on zoning and building code reform.

you've constructed a false dichotomy here.

the government of a city with ~8 million people is capable of doing multiple things at the same time.

sentences.en: > The energy would be better spent on zoning and building code reform.you've constructed a false dichotomy here.the government of a city with ~8 million people is capable of doing multiple things at the same time.
sentences.zh: > 最好把精力花在分区和建筑规范改革上。你在这里构建了一个错误的二分法。一个拥有约 800 万人口的城市的政府有能力同时做多项事情。

blocks.html_en:
The law applies to everyone, so it's reasonable for everyone to try to understand it, not just attorneys.

Similarly, it's fine for people to have opinions on food, dental hygiene, and the tax code without being a chef, a dentist, and an accountant.

sentences.en: The law applies to everyone, so it's reasonable for everyone to try to understand it, not just attorneys.Similarly, it's fine for people to have opinions on food, dental hygiene, and the tax code without being a chef, a dentist, and an accountant.
sentences.zh: 法律适用于每个人，因此每个人都试图理解它是合理的，而不仅仅是律师。同样，人们即使不是厨师、牙医和会计师，也可以对食物、牙齿卫生和税法有意见。

I thought it was illegal in new york (and probably the rest of the us) to give legal advice if not a lawyer.

And in my understanding interpreting the law as opposed to just reciting it constitutes legal advice.

blocks.html_en:
No, merely analyzing or discussing the law generally does not constitute the practice of law. If it did, plenty of newspaper articles, law review journals (often written by law students), non-attorney legal aid, and legal pamphlets would be prohibited.

If you want something more concrete, I googled it, and in New York, there appears to have been a case New York County Lawyers’ Association v. Dacey, in which Dacey wrote a book "How to Avoid Probate!", and the NYCA accepted a dissent in a lower court, stating in part:

    Does the writing, publication, advertising, sale and distribution of "How To Avoid Probate!" constitute the unauthorized practice of law within the meaning of subdivision B of section 750? It cannot be claimed that the publication of a legal text which purports to say what the law is amounts to legal practice. And the mere fact that the principles or rules stated in the text may be accepted by a particular reader as a solution to his problem does not affect this.

and later humorously quoting:

    "[I]t is a prized American privilege to speak one's mind, although not always with perfect good taste, on all public institutions" (Bridges v. California, 314 U. S. 252, 270)

https://accessiblelaw.org/Disclaimer.html

sentences.zh: 不，仅仅分析或讨论法律通常并不构成法律实践。

sentences.en: Dacey, in which Dacey wrote a book "How to Avoid Probate!", and the NYCA accepted a dissent in a lower court, stating in part: Does the writing, publication, advertising, sale and distribution of "How To Avoid Probate!"
sentences.zh: 达西，其中达西写了一本书“如何避免遗嘱认证！”，NYCA 在下级法院接受了异议，部分声明：“如何避免遗嘱认证！”的写作、出版、广告、销售和发行是否有效？

sentences.en: constitute the unauthorized practice of law within the meaning of subdivision B of section 750?
sentences.zh: 是否构成第 750 条 B 款含义内的未经授权的法律执业？

sentences.zh: 不能声称发布旨在说明法律是什么的法律文本就等于法律实践。

blocks.html_en:
I am honestly so surprised that everyone on HN is so naive that they take political statements like this at face value.

Politicians routinely say they will do things they do not have the authority to do, and it's often very important to understanding what will actually happen to have some understanding of what authorities are available to them, or at the very least ask Google/LLMs about it.

sentences.en: I am honestly so surprised that everyone on HN is so naive that they take political statements like this at face value.Politicians routinely say they will do things they do not have the authority to do, and it's often very important to understanding what will actually happen to have some understanding of what authorities are available to them, or at the very least ask Google/LLMs about it.
sentences.zh: 老实说，我很惊讶 HN 上的每个人都如此天真，他们只相信这样的政治声明的表面价值。政客们经常说他们会做他们没有权力做的事情，了解实际情况通常非常重要，了解当局可以为他们提供什么，或者至少询问谷歌/法学硕士。

blocks.html_en: Politicians routinely say they will do things they do not have the authority to do, and it's often very important to understanding what will actually happen to have some understanding of what authorities are available to them, or at the very least ask Google/LLMs about it.

sentences.en: Politicians routinely say they will do things they do not have the authority to do, and it's often very important to understanding what will actually happen to have some understanding of what authorities are available to them, or at the very least ask Google/LLMs about it.
sentences.zh: 政客们经常说他们会做他们无权做的事情，了解实际会发生什么通常非常重要，了解他们可以使用哪些权限，或者至少向谷歌/法学硕士询问相关情况。

blocks.html_en:
What do you call a reverse slippery-slope argument? “All images are edited, therefore ai editing is ok.”

Degrees of alteration matter, pretending ai images are the same as color retouching is dumb.

sentences.en: What do you call a reverse slippery-slope argument?
sentences.zh: 你所谓的反向滑坡论证是什么？

sentences.en: “All images are edited, therefore ai editing is ok.”Degrees of alteration matter, pretending ai images are the same as color retouching is dumb.
sentences.zh: “所有图像都经过编辑，因此人工智能编辑是可以的。”改变的程度很重要，假装人工智能图像与色彩修饰相同是愚蠢的。

blocks.html_en:
I strongly disagree. This is just the Politician's Fallacy.

I keep harping on about the "virtual staging" that real estate agents have been doing for a decade that is equally deceptive and annoying and already gets labeled, and the labels don't actually help because you're still left trying to decipher what is real yourself.

If they wanted to actually do something useful, they'd get together with the legislature and pass a law saying that real estate listings need to come with floor plans that are accurate within X% under the penalty of some sort of fine with a private right of action. But passing laws is hard and faces opposition.

sentences.en: I strongly disagree.
sentences.zh: 我强烈不同意。

sentences.en: This is just the Politician's Fallacy.I keep harping on about the "virtual staging" that real estate agents have been doing for a decade that is equally deceptive and annoying and already gets labeled, and the labels don't actually help because you're still left trying to decipher what is real yourself.If they wanted to actually do something useful, they'd get together with the legislature and pass a law saying that real estate listings need to come with floor plans that are accurate within X% under the penalty of some sort of fine with a private right of action.
sentences.zh: 这只是政治家的谬论。我一直在谈论房地产经纪人十年来一直在做的“虚拟舞台”，它同样具有欺骗性和烦人性，并且已经被贴上了标签，而且这些标签实际上并没有帮助，因为你仍然试图自己破译什么是真实的。如果他们想真正做一些有用的事情，他们会与立法机关会面并通过一项法律，规定房地产清单需要提供准确度在 X% 以内的平面图受到某种具有私人诉讼权的罚款的处罚。

sentences.en: But passing laws is hard and faces opposition.
sentences.zh: 但通过法律很困难并且面临反对。

blocks.html_en:
I am so certain of this because I was not born yesterday and this is not my first time paying attention to (NYC) politics.

Politician makes a grand statement they do not have the authority to meaningfully act on to get headlines, DCWP issues a weak sauce disclosure rule and the news cycle moves on because this is not actually anybody's priority.

sentences.en: I am so certain of this because I was not born yesterday and this is not my first time paying attention to (NYC) politics.Politician makes a grand statement they do not have the authority to meaningfully act on to get headlines, DCWP issues a weak sauce disclosure rule and the news cycle moves on because this is not actually anybody's priority.

blocks.html_en:
There's several other areas that would be good to categorically ban AI usage from:

  - gambling
  - dating
  - hiring
  - advertising

It shouldn't even be controversial that this would be broadly good for society.

I say that as an AI maximalist: I fully trust AI with these things. I do not trust the humans using the AI.

sentences.en: There's several other areas that would be good to categorically ban AI usage from: - gambling - dating - hiring - advertising It shouldn't even be controversial that this would be broadly good for society.I say that as an AI maximalist: I fully trust AI with these things.
sentences.zh: 还有其他几个领域可以明确禁止人工智能的使用： - 赌博 - 约会 - 招聘 - 广告 这对社会广泛有利，甚至不应该引起争议。作为一个人工智能最大化主义者，我说：我完全信任人工智能在这些方面的能力。

sentences.en: I do not trust the humans using the AI.
sentences.zh: 我不信任使用人工智能的人类。

blocks.html_en: Perhaps we need OP to explain "I fully trust AI with these things", because it is difficult to define trust in this sense. Yes of course the human motivation was always going to be the real "AI threat", and fraud (and a hugely increased spam volume) was always going to be the main route of AI damage to society.

sentences.en: Perhaps we need OP to explain "I fully trust AI with these things", because it is difficult to define trust in this sense.
sentences.zh: 也许我们需要 OP 来解释“我完全信任 AI 做这些事情”，因为很难从这个意义上定义信任。

sentences.en: Yes of course the human motivation was always going to be the real "AI threat", and fraud (and a hugely increased spam volume) was always going to be the main route of AI damage to society.
sentences.zh: 是的，当然，人类的动机永远是真正的“人工智能威胁”，而欺诈（以及垃圾邮件数量的大幅增加）永远是人工智能对社会造成损害的主要途径。

sentences.en: Yes of course the human motivation was always going to be the real "AI threat", and fraud (and a hugely increased spam volume) was always going to be the main route of AI damage to society.
sentences.zh: 是的，当然，人类的动机永远是真正的“人工智能威胁”，而欺诈（以及垃圾邮件数量的大幅增加）永远是人工智能对社会造成损害的主要途径。

blocks.html_en: Most real estate listing using a type of "HDR" exposure stacking due to the difficulty in taking photos of rooms that exposure the interior correctly and also expose the view from the windows in the same photo. It doesn't show things that aren't there, and personally I see it as acceptable, but I could see some law accidentally making it illegal.

blocks.html_en:
Advertising would be fine if they checked it. Food advertising has used fake images forever, but it's okay because you know the meatballs don't look like that. You have an idea of what warm fries taste like without the fake heat imagery.

But the dress you get is not the same as the dress in the picture. If the model looked like you, the dress should fit the same, but the AI dresses don't. Same figure, same skin color, same height, and yet the dress looks different.

That's the problem with homes. There's no way a room fits that many things but AI will make it look like it does. There's a distortion where it changes the specification entirely.

It's like showing someone playing Fallout 4 on a MacBook Air. It's a deceptive practice, unlike the cereal boxes showing milk.

sentences.en: Advertising would be fine if they checked it.
sentences.zh: 如果他们检查一下的话，广告就很好了。

sentences.en: Food advertising has used fake images forever, but it's okay because you know the meatballs don't look like that.
sentences.zh: 食品广告一直使用假图片，但这没关系，因为你知道肉丸看起来不是那样的。

sentences.en: You have an idea of what warm fries taste like without the fake heat imagery.But the dress you get is not the same as the dress in the picture.
sentences.zh: 在没有假热图像的情况下，您知道热薯条的味道。但是您得到的衣服与图片中的衣服不一样。

sentences.en: If the model looked like you, the dress should fit the same, but the AI dresses don't.
sentences.zh: 如果模特看起来像你，那么裙子应该是一样合身的，但 AI 裙子却不合身。

sentences.en: Same figure, same skin color, same height, and yet the dress looks different.That's the problem with homes.
sentences.zh: 同样的身材，同样的肤色，同样的身高，衣服却看起来不一样，这就是家的问题。

sentences.en: There's no way a room fits that many things but AI will make it look like it does.
sentences.zh: 一个房间不可能容纳那么多东西，但人工智能会让它看起来像这样。

sentences.en: There's a distortion where it changes the specification entirely.It's like showing someone playing Fallout 4 on a MacBook Air.
sentences.zh: 存在一种失真，它完全改变了规格。这就像显示某人在 MacBook Air 上玩《辐射 4》一样。

sentences.en: It's a deceptive practice, unlike the cereal boxes showing milk.
sentences.zh: 这是一种欺骗性的做法，与展示牛奶的麦片盒不同。

blocks.html_en:
> Food advertising has used fake images forever, but it's okay because you know the meatballs don't look like that.

Highly disagree. Color retouch or ambience or effects, sure. But changing shape, size, base color and perhaps some other things is deceit. Japan can do this, other countries should able too

blocks.html_en:
I agree with the goals of the policy but I don't understand how it's enforced.

How do you determine if something is enhanced by AI versus just Photoshop or something else? Apart from being physically impossible, I suppose.

blocks.html_en: I think advertising can be done right. Stack Overflow seems to have gotten it right, so much so that I disabled my adblocker for them. What bites me is when the advertisements are for something toward my caveman brain, like being advertised alcohol products while scrolling through instagram. I'd like the ability to say: "yes tell me about the newest raspberry pi products, but don't keep advertising gambling services".

blocks.html_en: Because every time something new comes along, people will push boundaries while arguing it is acceptable. In this case, they may argue that it is no different from physically furnishing an apartment, taking some photos, then removing the furniture. At least in terms of representing the product. Clearly using AI is much easier and cheaper than physically furnishing the apartment for a couple of hours. Some may even genuinely believe this, seeing it as more a tool of convenience than something that doesn't always represent physical reality.

blocks.html_en:
That doesn't answer the question. If you used photoshop's content-aware fill (introduced over a decade ago) to hide imperfections in your apartment, that would still be deceptive advertising. Moreover it's almost as easy as asking AI to do it, so the "AI makes everything easier" excuse doesn't work either.

I think the reason is clear. Politicians love to enact bills for already illegal things, but tailored for the current thing. In this case, it's AI, which there's bipartisan opposition. It makes them look responsive to their constituents and requires no political capital, because it's uncontroversial.

sentences.en: That doesn't answer the question.
sentences.zh: 这并不能回答问题。

sentences.en: If you used photoshop's content-aware fill (introduced over a decade ago) to hide imperfections in your apartment, that would still be deceptive advertising.
sentences.zh: 如果您使用 Photoshop 的内容感知填充（十多年前推出）来隐藏公寓中的缺陷，那仍然是欺骗性广告。

sentences.en: Moreover it's almost as easy as asking AI to do it, so the "AI makes everything easier" excuse doesn't work either.I think the reason is clear.
sentences.zh: 而且这几乎和要求人工智能来做一样容易，所以“人工智能让一切变得更容易”的借口也不起作用。我认为原因很清楚。

sentences.zh: 政客们喜欢为已经非法的事情制定法案，但又针对当前的事情量身定制。

sentences.en: In this case, it's AI, which there's bipartisan opposition.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，是人工智能，但遭到了两党的反对。

sentences.zh: 这让他们看起来对选民有回应，而且不需要政治资本，因为这是没有争议的。

blocks.html_en: It's not just good PR for politicians to pass "<specific instance> case of already illegal <generic> is explicitly illegal"-laws. Prosecuting a violation of <generic> tends to be a lot harder because in addition to providing evidence for <specific instance> it requires convincing a judge that <generic> even applies to <specific instance> in the first place. You get to short-circuit that ordeal by passing a law that clarifies the intent that yes, <specific instance> is in fact an instance of <generic> without having to go all the way through setting precedent in higher courts.

blocks.html_en:
The whole point of staging furniture is to help visualize and contextualize. Seeing a staged bed, couch, coffee table etc gives you a picture of how large or cramped the room is. AI furniture in contrast isn't limited by physics or reality and does not assist in showing the size of a room. It's only purpose is to deceive the viewer.

AR visualizations where virtual models that are true to real world furniture is much more acceptable.

sentences.en: The whole point of staging furniture is to help visualize and contextualize.
sentences.zh: 布置家具的全部目的是帮助可视化和情境化。

sentences.en: Seeing a staged bed, couch, coffee table etc gives you a picture of how large or cramped the room is.
sentences.zh: 看到一张布置好的床、沙发、咖啡桌等，你就会知道房间有多大或多狭窄。

sentences.en: AI furniture in contrast isn't limited by physics or reality and does not assist in showing the size of a room.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，人工智能家具不受物理或现实的限制，也无助于显示房间的大小。

sentences.en: It's only purpose is to deceive the viewer.AR visualizations where virtual models that are true to real world furniture is much more acceptable.
sentences.zh: 它的唯一目的是欺骗观众。AR 可视化，其中真实家具的虚拟模型更容易被接受。

blocks.html_en:
It's exactly because there are laws against deceptive advertising that Mamdani can enact theses rules.

NYC's Administrative Code prohibits deceptive trade practices, false advertising, misleading representations made to customers, etc. It gives the NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection authority to execute those broad guidelines by enacting specific rules.

So Mamdani and the DCWP are basically saying, "City law gives us the authority to regulate this sort of thing, and because this is clearly in violation, here are the specific rules we're enacting to regulate it."

sentences.en: It's exactly because there are laws against deceptive advertising that Mamdani can enact theses rules.NYC's Administrative Code prohibits deceptive trade practices, false advertising, misleading representations made to customers, etc. It gives the NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection authority to execute those broad guidelines by enacting specific rules.So Mamdani and the DCWP are basically saying, "City law gives us the authority to regulate this sort of thing, and because this is clearly in violation, here are the specific rules we're enacting to regulate it."
sentences.zh: 正是因为有针对欺骗性广告的法律，Mamdani 才能制定这些规则。《纽约市行政法规》禁止欺骗性贸易行为、虚假广告、向客户作出误导性陈述等。它授权纽约市消费者和工人保护部通过制定具体规则来执行这些广泛的指导方针。因此，Mamdani 和 DCWP 基本上是在说，“市法赋予我们监管此类行为的权力，因为这显然是违法的，这里是我们正在制定具体规则来对其进行监管。”

sentences.zh: 当然，他们有免责声明（大多数时候），但在缩略图大小的主图像中，不可能看到它是人工智能。

sentences.en: Which leads to clicking on a complete BS listing.
sentences.zh: 这导致点击完整的 BS 列表。

blocks.html_en:
Well we've also decided we don't like enforcing the law. So we've got the worst outcome, theft, fraud, and general lawlessness everywhere and nobody willing to do anything about it.

And in this case, no I don't think a footnote "some photos may contain AI enhancements" at the bottom of the page in a low-contrast color is helpful.

sentences.en: Well we've also decided we don't like enforcing the law.

sentences.zh: 因此，我们遇到了最糟糕的结果，盗窃、欺诈和普遍的无法无天现象无处不在，没有人愿意对此采取任何行动。在这种情况下，不，我不认为页面底部的低对比度颜色脚注“某些照片可能包含人工智能增强功能”有帮助。

blocks.html_en: I would personally love to see an end to the universally existing bait-and-switch of brokers listing unavailable units just to get you on the phone, then when you contact them, saying "Oh sorry that unit just got rented (or sold), but, I have another one that might suit you"

sentences.zh: 我个人希望看到经纪人普遍存在的诱饵和转换的结束，只是为了让你打电话，然后当你联系他们时，说“哦，抱歉，该单位刚刚被租用（或出售），但是，我有另一套可能适合你”

blocks.html_en: To begin with, they should make it a legal requirement to list Condo vs Coop in listings, not "condo/coop". One is usually easier to get, the other pushes people thru discriminatory hoops that many profiles have no hopes of passing -- but get stuck in the theatre of process and formality (often not knowing the entire process is engineered to exclude them.)

sentences.en: To begin with, they should make it a legal requirement to list Condo vs Coop in listings, not "condo/coop".
sentences.zh: 首先，他们应该将在列表中列出公寓与合作社而不是“公寓/合作社”作为法律要求。

sentences.en: One is usually easier to get, the other pushes people thru discriminatory hoops that many profiles have no hopes of passing -- but get stuck in the theatre of process and formality (often not knowing the entire process is engineered to exclude them.)
sentences.zh: 一种通常更容易获得，另一种则将人们推入歧视性的困境，许多个人资料都没有希望通过这些障碍，但却陷入了流程和形式的困境（通常不知道整个流程的设计就是为了将他们排除在外）。

blocks.html_en: This goes beyond AI its about reducing information asymmetry in one of the largest markets in the economy. The markets work better when buyers and renters can precisely assess what they're paying for. AI just makes mis representation cheaper and more scalable.

sentences.en: This goes beyond AI its about reducing information asymmetry in one of the largest markets in the economy.
sentences.zh: 这超出了人工智能的范围，它是为了减少经济中最大市场之一的信息不对称。

sentences.en: The markets work better when buyers and renters can precisely assess what they're paying for.
sentences.zh: 当买家和租户能够准确评估他们所支付的费用时，市场就会更好地运作。

sentences.en: AI just makes mis representation cheaper and more scalable.
sentences.zh: 人工智能只会让错误表述变得更便宜、更具可扩展性。

blocks.html_en: Facebook marketplace driving me crazy lately. A lot of people post photos of antiques and other furniture with obvious AI staging. It’s hard to tell what is real and what isn’t. At least there I can just demand the normal photos. I know that isn’t the case with most rentals in NYC because it’s super competitive and already gated in ridiculous ways with brokers and real estate agents.

blocks.html_en:
While we're at it, this should cover products and food in general too. I hate it when I see a poster that clearly has AI-generated images of food and the actual food you get is nowhere near what they generated.

Or even if it's similar, it's still clearly staged to look more appealing than what you should be getting.

Everyone knows deceptive advertising is bad, but somehow those who use AI for advertising turned their brains off on this topic because the images looked cooler than what they'd normally produce with a camera.

sentences.en: While we're at it, this should cover products and food in general too.
sentences.zh: 当我们这样做时，这也应该涵盖一般的产品和食品。

sentences.en: I hate it when I see a poster that clearly has AI-generated images of food and the actual food you get is nowhere near what they generated.Or even if it's similar, it's still clearly staged to look more appealing than what you should be getting.Everyone knows deceptive advertising is bad, but somehow those who use AI for advertising turned their brains off on this topic because the images looked cooler than what they'd normally produce with a camera.
sentences.zh: 当我看到一张海报上明显有人工智能生成的食物图像，而你得到的实际食物与它们生成的食物相差甚远时，我讨厌它。或者即使它很相似，它仍然明显地看起来比你应该得到的更有吸引力。每个人都知道欺骗性广告是不好的，但不知何故，那些使用人工智能做广告的人却对这个话题不感兴趣，因为这些图像看起来比他们通常用相机生成的图像更酷。

blocks.html_en:
While we're at it, this should cover products and food in general too. I hate it when I see a poster that clearly has AI-generated images of food and the actual food you get is nowhere near what they generated.

Or even if it's similar, it's still clearly staged to look more appealing than what you should be getting.

Everyone knows deceptive advertising is bad, but somehow those who use AI for advertising turned their brains off on this topic because the images looked cooler than what they'd normally produce with a camera.

sentences.en: While we're at it, this should cover products and food in general too.
sentences.zh: 当我们这样做时，这也应该涵盖一般的产品和食品。

sentences.en: I hate it when I see a poster that clearly has AI-generated images of food and the actual food you get is nowhere near what they generated.Or even if it's similar, it's still clearly staged to look more appealing than what you should be getting.Everyone knows deceptive advertising is bad, but somehow those who use AI for advertising turned their brains off on this topic because the images looked cooler than what they'd normally produce with a camera.
sentences.zh: 当我看到一张海报上明显有人工智能生成的食物图像，而你得到的实际食物与它们生成的食物相差甚远时，我讨厌它。或者即使它很相似，它仍然明显地看起来比你应该得到的更有吸引力。每个人都知道欺骗性广告是不好的，但不知何故，那些使用人工智能做广告的人却对这个话题不感兴趣，因为这些图像看起来比他们通常用相机生成的图像更酷。

blocks.html_en: You realize that food photography for decades essentially did the same thing? No Burger ever served at McDonalds has ever matched the looks of their advertising, nor is the actual prop-thing that was photographed remotely eatable. There exists an entire form of mastery around this work.

blocks.html_en:
Food photography is regulated; they have to show the actual product being sold.

They just pay a lot more attention to the presentation; pick the nicest bit of lettuce, the ripest tomato, place everything with care.

Stuff you aren't selling can be props, like Elmer's glue for milk in a cereal ad. It won't fly in a milk ad, though.

sentences.en: Food photography is regulated; they have to show the actual product being sold.They just pay a lot more attention to the presentation; pick the nicest bit of lettuce, the ripest tomato, place everything with care.Stuff you aren't selling can be props, like Elmer's glue for milk in a cereal ad.
sentences.zh: 食品摄影受到监管；他们必须展示正在销售的实际产品。他们只是更加注重展示；挑选最好的生菜，最熟的西红柿，小心地放置所有东西。你不出售的东西可以是道具，就像谷物广告中埃尔默的牛奶胶水一样。

sentences.en: It won't fly in a milk ad, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，它不会出现在牛奶广告中。

blocks.html_en:
It depends on the municipality.

Usually, local laws are enacted by a city council with members elected to represent different areas of a municipality (and potentially some at-large seats). Legislation can come from a number of places: lobbying efforts (for example, there's a current effort for municipalities to ban data center development), citizen groups, specially formed committees (my city just had one to create and propose city charter revisions), or yes, occasionally the council members themselves. Typically, there are open council meetings where citizens can discuss their opinions.

In addition, some rules/regulations/taxes are directly put to a vote of the city/town population, and some regulations can be put in place by administrative departments in a locality. (Similar to things like EPA regulations on the federal level).

The specific implementations vary in accordance to municipal charters and how specific states handle the incorporation of local governments.

sentences.en: It depends on the municipality.Usually, local laws are enacted by a city council with members elected to represent different areas of a municipality (and potentially some at-large seats).
sentences.zh: 这取决于市政府。通常，地方法律由市议会制定，选出的成员代表市政府的不同区域（可能还有一些普通席位）。

sentences.en: Legislation can come from a number of places: lobbying efforts (for example, there's a current effort for municipalities to ban data center development), citizen groups, specially formed committees (my city just had one to create and propose city charter revisions), or yes, occasionally the council members themselves.
sentences.zh: 立法可以来自多个地方：游说工作（例如，目前市政府正在努力禁止数据中心的开发）、公民团体、专门成立的委员会（我的城市刚刚成立了一个委员会来制定和提议修订城市章程），或者是的，有时是市议会成员自己。

sentences.en: Typically, there are open council meetings where citizens can discuss their opinions.In addition, some rules/regulations/taxes are directly put to a vote of the city/town population, and some regulations can be put in place by administrative departments in a locality.
sentences.zh: 通常，有公开的理事会会议，公民可以讨论他们的意见。此外，一些规则/法规/税收直接由城市/城镇人口投票，有些法规可以由当地行政部门制定。

sentences.en: (Similar to things like EPA regulations on the federal level).The specific implementations vary in accordance to municipal charters and how specific states handle the incorporation of local governments.
sentences.zh: （类似于联邦层面的 EPA 法规）。具体实施方式根据市政章程以及具体州如何处理地方政府的合并而有所不同。

sentences.en: Typically, there are open council meetings where citizens can discuss their opinions.In addition, some rules/regulations/taxes are directly put to a vote of the city/town population, and some regulations can be put in place by administrative departments in a locality.
sentences.zh: 通常，有公开的理事会会议，公民可以讨论他们的意见。此外，一些规则/法规/税收直接由城市/城镇人口投票，有些法规可以由当地行政部门制定。

sentences.en: (Similar to things like EPA regulations on the federal level).The specific implementations vary in accordance to municipal charters and how specific states handle the incorporation of local governments.
sentences.zh: （类似于联邦层面的 EPA 法规）。具体实施方式根据市政章程以及具体州如何处理地方政府的合并而有所不同。

blocks.html_en: It depends on the municipality.

sentences.en: It depends on the municipality.
sentences.zh: 这取决于市政府。

blocks.html_en: Usually, local laws are enacted by a city council with members elected to represent different areas of a municipality (and potentially some at-large seats). Legislation can come from a number of places: lobbying efforts (for example, there's a current effort for municipalities to ban data center development), citizen groups, specially formed committees (my city just had one to create and propose city charter revisions), or yes, occasionally the council members themselves. Typically, there are open council meetings where citizens can discuss their opinions.

blocks.html_en:
Cities have city councils that pass laws.

This likely doesn't even require a new law. There is probably an existing law against deceptive advertising in renting. This is just the mayor announcing that he will interpret the existing law to cover AI generated staging images.

blocks.html_en:
well, reading the article Mamdani is cracking down on "deceptive landlord practices" thus it means his administration will apply deceptive landlord practice laws to use of AI images in advertising apartments. At some point if somebody wants to fight the issue they can take it to court.

As a general rule you probably don't need new laws to penalize behavior you think should be penalized, there are more than enough laws where a good faith interpretation would fit.

blocks.html_en:
I'm not sure why you're downvoted. Many cities have a housing department and they can write and update regulations and requirements (within the scope of their legislatively-granted authority) that have the force of law. Things get set up this way so legislative bodies don't have to write and vote on every detail of every rule.

It's possible someone might challenge a rule if they think it oversteps the authority granted.

sentences.en: I'm not sure why you're downvoted.

sentences.zh: 许多城市都有住房部门，他们可以制定和更新具有法律效力的法规和要求（在其立法授权的范围内）。

sentences.zh: 事情以这种方式建立，因此立法机构不必对每条规则的每个细节进行起草和投票。如果有人认为某条规则超出了授予的权力，他们可能会质疑该规则。

blocks.html_en:
yeah me neither, maybe it was using the phrase good faith.

I suppose landlords if they think it is very beneficial to use AI to get people to pay more for apartments might fight back, probably free speech or some such thing, some landlords might just do it because they dislike Mamdani.

Anyway I'm not sure if they would need to update much, just issue statement "using AI to create an image that cannot actually happen in reality for an apartment by.. (long winded description follows) is obviously deceptive and falls under current regulations and laws and we will be prosecuting it as such" - this would of course be determined by how things work in NY specifically.

blocks.html_en:
I think it is basically just signaling to the county DA's as to what they ought to consider when seeking out blatant cases of rental fraud; the laws already exist in the deceptive practices code...

I think an actual law does have to be passed to enact the part literally banning all AI imagery on a five boroughs basis, as opposed to just penalizing inaccurate AI genned imagery... which afaik is municipality based. Pretty sure the City Council needs to codify that.

Not sure who would be responsible for enforcing it on pretty much every site in the world that isn't just the real estate broker or building management/etc, though. Would places like rent.com be legally responsible?

sentences.en: I think it is basically just signaling to the county DA's as to what they ought to consider when seeking out blatant cases of rental fraud; the laws already exist in the deceptive practices code...I think an actual law does have to be passed to enact the part literally banning all AI imagery on a five boroughs basis, as opposed to just penalizing inaccurate AI genned imagery...
sentences.zh: 我认为这基本上只是向县地方检察官发出信号，告诉他们在寻找公然的租赁欺诈案件时应该考虑什么；法律已经存在于欺骗行为守则中……我认为确实必须通过一项实际的法律来颁布在五个行政区的基础上禁止所有人工智能图像的部分，而不是仅仅惩罚不准确的人工智能生成的图像……

sentences.en: which afaik is municipality based.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，这是一个市政府。

sentences.en: Pretty sure the City Council needs to codify that.Not sure who would be responsible for enforcing it on pretty much every site in the world that isn't just the real estate broker or building management/etc, though.
sentences.zh: 很确定市议会需要对此进行编纂。不过，不确定谁将负责在世界上几乎每个地点执行该规定，而不仅仅是房地产经纪人或建筑管理等。

sentences.en: Would places like rent.com be legally responsible?
sentences.zh: 像rent.com这样的地方会承担法律责任吗？

sentences.en: which afaik is municipality based.

blocks.html_en:
What’s the point though? To save prospective renters time?

I’ve lived here for 30+ years, rented for more than 20 and why would anyone ever rent an apartment without seeing it in person?

That being said, IANAL but I imagine the rule is fully legal. The city already mandates a host of things: if the listing markets something as a 3BR, it needs to have 3 rooms bigger than 80 sq feet, each with an exterior window. If they say 3BR and it needs a wall to created the 3rd BR they have to put it up. If it says 2BR convertible 3BR, you might have to pay to have it put up.

sentences.en: What’s the point though?
sentences.zh: 但有什么意义呢？

sentences.en: To save prospective renters time?I’ve lived here for 30+ years, rented for more than 20 and why would anyone ever rent an apartment without seeing it in person?That being said, IANAL but I imagine the rule is fully legal.
sentences.zh: 为了节省潜在租房者的时间？我在这里住了 30 多年，租了 20 多年，为什么有人会在没有亲自看过公寓的情况下租一套公寓呢？话虽如此，IANAL 但我认为这条规则是完全合法的。

sentences.en: The city already mandates a host of things: if the listing markets something as a 3BR, it needs to have 3 rooms bigger than 80 sq feet, each with an exterior window.
sentences.zh: 该市已经规定了很多要求：如果挂牌的商品是 3 居室，则需要有 3 个面积超过 80 平方英尺的房间，每个房间都有外窗。

sentences.en: If they say 3BR and it needs a wall to created the 3rd BR they have to put it up.
sentences.zh: 如果他们说 3BR 并且需要一堵墙来创建第三个 BR，他们就必须把它竖起来。

sentences.en: If it says 2BR convertible 3BR, you might have to pay to have it put up.
sentences.zh: 如果上面写着 2BR 可转换 3BR，您可能需要付费才能安装。

blocks.html_en: That kind of regulation has failed over and over. The obvious outcome is that every listing will have misleading AI "photos".

sentences.en: The obvious outcome is that every listing will have misleading AI "photos".

blocks.html_en:
Define "failed".

If what ends up happening is that every listing has misleading AI photos but they have to disclose it, then also what ends up happening is nobody trusts them anymore. Consumers will know by default to not trust the photos.

In my eyes, thats a win since that's a better outcome than them secretly using AI photos.

Of course in my ideal world it would be outlawed altogether, But even if they were still allowed to use AI photos but were forced to disclose it, that's still a good first step.

sentences.en: Define "failed".If what ends up happening is that every listing has misleading AI photos but they have to disclose it, then also what ends up happening is nobody trusts them anymore.
sentences.zh: 定义“失败”。如果最终发生的情况是每个列表都有误导性的人工智能照片但他们必须披露它，那么最终发生的情况就是没人再信任他们。

sentences.en: Consumers will know by default to not trust the photos.In my eyes, thats a win since that's a better outcome than them secretly using AI photos.Of course in my ideal world it would be outlawed altogether, But even if they were still allowed to use AI photos but were forced to disclose it, that's still a good first step.
sentences.zh: 消费者会默认知道不要相信这些照片。在我看来，这是一个胜利，因为这比他们秘密使用人工智能照片更好。当然，在我的理想世界中，它会被完全禁止，但即使他们仍然被允许使用人工智能照片，但被迫披露，这仍然是一个很好的第一步。

blocks.html_en:
Define "failed".

If what ends up happening is that every listing has misleading AI photos but they have to disclose it, then also what ends up happening is nobody trusts them anymore. Consumers will know by default to not trust the photos.

In my eyes, thats a win since that's a better outcome than them secretly using AI photos.

Of course in my ideal world it would be outlawed altogether, But even if they were still allowed to use AI photos but were forced to disclose it, that's still a good first step.

sentences.zh: 定义“失败”。如果最终发生的情况是每个列表都有误导性的人工智能照片但他们必须披露它，那么最终发生的情况就是没人再信任他们。

sentences.en: Consumers will know by default to not trust the photos.In my eyes, thats a win since that's a better outcome than them secretly using AI photos.Of course in my ideal world it would be outlawed altogether, But even if they were still allowed to use AI photos but were forced to disclose it, that's still a good first step.
sentences.zh: 消费者会默认知道不要相信这些照片。在我看来，这是一个胜利，因为这比他们秘密使用人工智能照片更好。当然，在我的理想世界中，它会被完全禁止，但即使他们仍然被允许使用人工智能照片，但被迫披露，这仍然是一个很好的第一步。

blocks.html_en:
What?

Is your claim that every photo will be labeled as AI-modified, or that people won't label AI-modified images? If the latter, just penalize the listing agents. Trivial.

The entire issue is that the platforms are already inundated with misleading, unlabeled AI-modified images.

sentences.en: What?Is your claim that every photo will be labeled as AI-modified, or that people won't label AI-modified images?

sentences.en: If the latter, just penalize the listing agents.
sentences.zh: 如果是后者，就惩罚上市代理吧。

sentences.en: Trivial.The entire issue is that the platforms are already inundated with misleading, unlabeled AI-modified images.
sentences.zh: 微不足道。整个问题在于，这些平台已经充斥着误导性的、未标记的人工智能修改图像。

blocks.html_en: It's not "if." My mother-in-law is a realtor. She has a storage locker full of furniture for staging. I guarantee that the monthly cost of that locker, plus the cost of moving the furniture in and out of every property is an order of magnitude more expensive than whatever tool is doing the AI fake staging. The cost savings are too attractive.

sentences.en: She has a storage locker full of furniture for staging.
sentences.zh: 她有一个储物柜，里面装满了用于舞台表演的家具。

sentences.en: I guarantee that the monthly cost of that locker, plus the cost of moving the furniture in and out of every property is an order of magnitude more expensive than whatever tool is doing the AI fake staging.

sentences.en: The cost savings are too attractive.
sentences.zh: 节省的成本太有吸引力了。

blocks.html_en:
> If the latter, just penalize the listing agents. Trivial.

It's very unlikely to be trivial though because the state typically lacks the resources required to enforce things like this at scale. You'll need to find violators, meet a burden of proof that they violated the law, notify them, give them the right to defend themsleves against the allegation, etc.

They'll almost certainly spend more time and money on the process than is ever collected if this ever happens.

sentences.en: > If the latter, just penalize the listing agents.
sentences.zh: > 如果是后者，只需处罚上市代理即可。

sentences.en: Trivial.It's very unlikely to be trivial though because the state typically lacks the resources required to enforce things like this at scale.
sentences.zh: 微不足道。但这不太可能是微不足道的，因为国家通常缺乏大规模执行此类事情所需的资源。

sentences.en: You'll need to find violators, meet a burden of proof that they violated the law, notify them, give them the right to defend themsleves against the allegation, etc.They'll almost certainly spend more time and money on the process than is ever collected if this ever happens.
sentences.zh: 您需要找到违法者，承担他们违反法律的举证责任，通知他们，赋予他们针对指控进行辩护的权利，等等。如果发生这种情况，他们几乎肯定会在这个过程中花费比以往收集到的更多的时间和金钱。

blocks.html_en:
> They'll almost certainly spend more time and money on the process than is ever collected if this ever happens.

The point of regulation isn't for the state to turn a profit. In fact, I'd go as far as to say that regulations that drive a monetary profit for the state are generally bad because they create a perverse incentive. For example, municipal governments adversely affect traffic flow by lowering speed limits because those lower speed limits generate more ticket revenue.

sentences.en: > They'll almost certainly spend more time and money on the process than is ever collected if this ever happens.The point of regulation isn't for the state to turn a profit.
sentences.zh: > 如果发生这种情况，他们几乎肯定会在这个过程中花费比以往收集到的更多的时间和金钱。监管的目的不是为了让国家盈利。

sentences.en: In fact, I'd go as far as to say that regulations that drive a monetary profit for the state are generally bad because they create a perverse incentive.
sentences.zh: 事实上，我什至可以说，为国家带来货币利润的监管通常是不好的，因为它们会产生不正当的激励。

sentences.en: For example, municipal governments adversely affect traffic flow by lowering speed limits because those lower speed limits generate more ticket revenue.
sentences.zh: 例如，市政府通过降低速度限制对交通流量产生不利影响，因为较低的速度限制会产生更多的门票收入。

blocks.html_en:
You're right that the point of regulation isn't to turn a "profit" but the laws of economics always apply. If you have a fine of $100 for a widespread practice that costs $1,000 to collect, the state isn't going to magically allocate resource to applying it.

You could create a private right of action for this, but that is its own bag of worms.

sentences.en: You're right that the point of regulation isn't to turn a "profit" but the laws of economics always apply.
sentences.zh: 你说得对，监管的目的不是为了获取“利润”，但经济规律始终适用。

sentences.en: If you have a fine of $100 for a widespread practice that costs $1,000 to collect, the state isn't going to magically allocate resource to applying it.You could create a private right of action for this, but that is its own bag of worms.
sentences.zh: 如果你因一项需要花费 1,000 美元收集的广泛做法而被处以 100 美元的罚款，国家不会神奇地分配资源来应用它。你可以为此创建一个私人诉讼权，但那是它自己的一袋蠕虫。

blocks.html_en:
You're right that the point of regulation isn't to turn a "profit" but the laws of economics always apply. If you have a fine of $100 for a widespread practice that costs $1,000 to collect, the state isn't going to magically allocate resource to applying it.

You could create a private right of action for this, but that is its own bag of worms.

> If you have a fine of $100 for a widespread practice that costs $1,000 to collect, the state isn't going to magically allocate resource to applying it.

Seems empirically false given e.g. the immense investment we make in counter-drug enforcement.

blocks.html_en:
> municipal governments adversely affect traffic flow by lowering speed limits because those lower speed limits generate more ticket revenue.

I don't know about the US because the US is weird, but:

* at 30kmph the rate of fatalities in case of a car hitting a pedestrian is basically 0%, at 50kmph I think it's 5% or more

* at 30kmph collisions are much easier to avoid due to the increased reaction time and the decreased braking distance (I don't remember the exact numbers)

* at 30kmph you can hold a conversation at normal speech levels next to a moderately busy road, at 50kmph you will have to shout (and not even notice it due to the high ambient noise)

Etc.

sentences.en: > municipal governments adversely affect traffic flow by lowering speed limits because those lower speed limits generate more ticket revenue.I don't know about the US because the US is weird, but:* at 30kmph the rate of fatalities in case of a car hitting a pedestrian is basically 0%, at 50kmph I think it's 5% or more* at 30kmph collisions are much easier to avoid due to the increased reaction time and the decreased braking distance (I don't remember the exact numbers)* at 30kmph you can hold a conversation at normal speech levels next to a moderately busy road, at 50kmph you will have to shout (and not even notice it due to the high ambient noise)Etc.
sentences.zh: > 市政府通过降低速度限制对交通流量产生不利影响，因为较低的速度限制会产生更多的罚单收入。我不了解美国，因为美国很奇怪，但是：*在 30 公里每小时时，汽车撞到行人的死亡率基本上是 0%，在 50 公里每小时我认为是 5% 或更多*在 30 公里每小时时，由于反应时间增加和制动距离缩短，碰撞更容易避免（我不记得确切的数字）*在 30 公里/小时的速度下，您可以在中等繁忙的道路旁以正常的语音水平进行对话，在 50 公里/小时的速度下，您将不得不大喊大叫（由于环境噪音很大，甚至没有注意到）等等。

Oh yeah, this tiny apartment can definitely fit all this furniture. It’s not all ai generated at 2/3rds the size of any real furniture.

blocks.html_en: Ethical edits would be stuff you couldn't notice in the inspection. The coffee cup is fine because in the inspection that won't be there and isn't a part of the property anyway. If you use AI to remove the rust and calcium on the taps that is something you can inspect and prove was modified in a misleading way.

blocks.html_en:
Requiring disclosure seems obvious.

Using AI for these pics is also not inherently deceptive though.

I live in an extremely overheated housing market where properties are usually sold/rented long before they actually get completed. I'm fine with landlords using AI in their renders to make claims about how the place will eventually look.

You also see people using AI to put furniture into the image (I assume they are also taking out the furniture that's actually there, belonging to the previous tenant, but doesn't fit their desired aesthetic). Again, nothing _inherently_ deceptive about this.

Main thing is just whether tenants are empowered to back out of the contract if they don't get what they were promised.

Anyone who e.g. uses AI to expand rooms/windows... Jail please.

sentences.en: Requiring disclosure seems obvious.Using AI for these pics is also not inherently deceptive though.I live in an extremely overheated housing market where properties are usually sold/rented long before they actually get completed.
sentences.zh: 要求披露似乎是显而易见的。不过，使用人工智能来拍摄这些照片本质上也不具有欺骗性。我生活在一个极其过热的房地产市场，房产通常在实际完工之前很久就被出售/出租。

sentences.en: I'm fine with landlords using AI in their renders to make claims about how the place will eventually look.You also see people using AI to put furniture into the image (I assume they are also taking out the furniture that's actually there, belonging to the previous tenant, but doesn't fit their desired aesthetic).
sentences.zh: 我同意房东在渲染中使用人工智能来声明该地方最终的外观。你还看到人们使用人工智能将家具放入图像中（我假设他们也取出了实际存在的家具，属于前一个租户，但不符合他们想要的审美）。

sentences.en: Again, nothing _inherently_ deceptive about this.Main thing is just whether tenants are empowered to back out of the contract if they don't get what they were promised.Anyone who e.g. uses AI to expand rooms/windows...
sentences.zh: 再说一次，这本身并没有什么欺骗性。主要的问题是，如果租户没有得到承诺，他们是否有权退出合同。使用人工智能来扩展房间/窗户......

sentences.en: Jail please.
sentences.zh: 请坐牢。

blocks.html_en: I am not a lawyer, but this seems unlikely. Federal law prohibits "false advertisement" which is understood to include misleading advertisements. Regulators can and do restrict certain types of commercial speech, and this kind of restriction has survived First Amendment challenge.

sentences.zh: 我不是律师，但这似乎不太可能。

sentences.en: Federal law prohibits "false advertisement" which is understood to include misleading advertisements.
sentences.zh: 联邦法律禁止“虚假广告”，其中包括误导性广告。

sentences.en: Regulators can and do restrict certain types of commercial speech, and this kind of restriction has survived First Amendment challenge.
sentences.zh: 监管机构可以而且确实限制某些类型的商业言论，而且这种限制经受住了第一修正案的挑战。

blocks.html_en:
Given that this would be the first ever law with any degree of ambiguity ever created, we should create some type of like... room... maybe call it a "court"... where people could "judge" whether a person fell on the allowable or disallowable end of the spectrum

It's a groundbreaking idea but it might work. And who knows, maybe it's an innovation we could apply to other areas of law in case they also ever need to interact with any ambiguity (which hasn't happened yet, of course).

sentences.en: Given that this would be the first ever law with any degree of ambiguity ever created, we should create some type of like...
sentences.zh: 鉴于这将是有史以来第一部具有任何程度模糊性的法律，我们应该创建某种类型的……

sentences.en: room...
sentences.zh: 房间...

sentences.en: maybe call it a "court"...
sentences.zh: 或许可以称之为“法庭”……

sentences.en: where people could "judge" whether a person fell on the allowable or disallowable end of the spectrumIt's a groundbreaking idea but it might work.
sentences.zh: 人们可以“判断”一个人是否属于允许或不允许的范围。这是一个开创性的想法，但它可能会起作用。

sentences.en: And who knows, maybe it's an innovation we could apply to other areas of law in case they also ever need to interact with any ambiguity (which hasn't happened yet, of course).
sentences.zh: 谁知道呢，也许这是我们可以应用于其他法律领域的创新，以防它们也需要与任何歧义进行交互（当然，这还没有发生）。

blocks.html_en:
no, the photos you take with the lenses on your phone are not AI generated. They are generated from the sensors on your phone.

Have you seen some of these listings? We are talking about retaining walls invented where they can’t exist, work displayed that hasn’t occurred, etc. if you show up to a property and it’s materially different than the picture that got you there, that should be illegal.

If you want to make an argument that “everything is AI now” go for it. But I’m happy to see existing false advertising laws evolve as technology evolves

sentences.en: no, the photos you take with the lenses on your phone are not AI generated.
sentences.zh: 不，您用手机镜头拍摄的照片不是人工智能生成的。

sentences.en: They are generated from the sensors on your phone.Have you seen some of these listings?

blocks.html_en:
Android phones sure. The iPhone as far as I have seen has stayed away from gimmicks and AI. "Computational photography" is a long shot away from AI generated images. Everything in the iphone photo actually existed much the same as the photo shows, it's just exposure and color being messed with.

If you use post editing tools like magic eraser and the new reframe / expand tools then that's a different story and shouldn't be allowed in real estate photography.

sentences.en: Android phones sure.
sentences.zh: 安卓手机肯定可以。

sentences.en: The iPhone as far as I have seen has stayed away from gimmicks and AI.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，iPhone 远离了噱头和人工智能。

sentences.en: "Computational photography" is a long shot away from AI generated images.
sentences.zh: “计算摄影”距离人工智能生成图像还很遥远。

sentences.en: Everything in the iphone photo actually existed much the same as the photo shows, it's just exposure and color being messed with.If you use post editing tools like magic eraser and the new reframe / expand tools then that's a different story and shouldn't be allowed in real estate photography.
sentences.zh: iPhone 照片中的所有内容实际上与照片显示的基本相同，只是曝光和颜色被弄乱了。如果您使用魔术橡皮擦等后期编辑工具和新的重新构图/扩展工具，那么这是一个不同的故事，不应该在房地产摄影中允许。

Love the guy but let’s try something that doesn’t restrict freedom of the press!

We love restricting our enemies, but there are better ways.

I propose banning rent at all!

blocks.html_en:
I'm assuming "AI images" means realtors using AI to stage empty rooms with furniture.

I'm honestly fine with that as long as it's labeled.

Having just done an apartment search a few months ago, AI staged images are surprisingly good quality. It's difficult to detect it as AI when going through a bunch of listings quickly. But yea, I guess it can cause confusion if it sticks a Peloton (or whatever) in a space where it won't actually fit.

sentences.en: I'm assuming "AI images" means realtors using AI to stage empty rooms with furniture.I'm honestly fine with that as long as it's labeled.Having just done an apartment search a few months ago, AI staged images are surprisingly good quality.
sentences.zh: 我假设“AI 图像”是指房地产经纪人使用 AI 来布置配有家具的空房间。老实说，只要有标签，我就可以接受。几个月前刚刚完成了一次公寓搜索，AI 布置的图像质量出奇的好。

sentences.en: It's difficult to detect it as AI when going through a bunch of listings quickly.
sentences.zh: 当快速浏览一堆列表时，很难将其检测为人工智能。

sentences.en: But yea, I guess it can cause confusion if it sticks a Peloton (or whatever) in a space where it won't actually fit.
sentences.zh: 但是，是的，我想如果它将 Peloton（或其他东西）粘在一个实际上不适合的空间中，可能会引起混乱。

blocks.html_en:
I just moved into a new apartment and tried using AI for layout inspiration. Every single attempt expanded the room, shrunk furniture, and even changed where walls were.

Landlords should not be using tools to stage units, it's going to lead to false expectations on the size of apartments.

sentences.en: I just moved into a new apartment and tried using AI for layout inspiration.
sentences.zh: 我刚刚搬进新公寓，尝试使用人工智能来获取布局灵感。

sentences.en: Every single attempt expanded the room, shrunk furniture, and even changed where walls were.Landlords should not be using tools to stage units, it's going to lead to false expectations on the size of apartments.
sentences.zh: 每一次尝试都扩大了房间，缩小了家具，甚至改变了墙壁的位置。房东不应该使用工具来布置单元，这会导致对公寓面积的错误预期。

sentences.en: Every single attempt expanded the room, shrunk furniture, and even changed where walls were.
sentences.zh: 每一次尝试都扩大了房间，缩小了家具，甚至改变了墙壁的位置。

blocks.html_en: Landlords should not be using tools to stage units, it's going to lead to false expectations on the size of apartments.

sentences.en: Landlords should not be using tools to stage units, it's going to lead to false expectations on the size of apartments.
sentences.zh: 房东不应该使用工具来安置单位，这会导致对公寓面积的错误预期。

blocks.html_en:
There are software tools made specifically for staging (and de-staging!) real estate photos. I don't know if they're using off-the-shelf image models or not, but they have capabilities like restricting changes to specific regions of the image which aren't available in services like ChatGPT.

(De-staging is a particularly neat trick - if a property still has some of the current tenant's belongings in it, an AI model can remove those items to show what the room would look like empty.)

sentences.en: There are software tools made specifically for staging (and de-staging!)
sentences.zh: 有一些专门用于登台（和取消登台！）的软件工具。

sentences.en: real estate photos.
sentences.zh: 房地产照片。

sentences.en: I don't know if they're using off-the-shelf image models or not, but they have capabilities like restricting changes to specific regions of the image which aren't available in services like ChatGPT.(De-staging is a particularly neat trick - if a property still has some of the current tenant's belongings in it, an AI model can remove those items to show what the room would look like empty.)
sentences.zh: 我不知道他们是否使用现成的图像模型，但他们具有限制对图像特定区域进行更改等功能，而这些功能在 ChatGPT 等服务中是不可用的。（取消暂存是一个特别巧妙的技巧 - 如果房产中仍然有当前租户的一些物品，人工智能模型可以删除这些物品，以显示房间看起来是空的。）

blocks.html_en:
The listings I saw with AI staging usually alternated photos, 1 photo unstaged, the next photo staged.

Which meant you could toggle between the staged and unstaged photo. I didn’t notice any warping or distortion.

sentences.zh: 我没有注意到任何扭曲或变形。

blocks.html_en: Yeah. With CAD models, every single trick I have tried to make photo mock-ups with an AI image-to-image conversion, whether using a line art or canny edge detector or just a shaded source object, has seen the AI ultimately ignore the cues in some generations, no matter what I do, and I would expect it to work a lot better with room photography.

sentences.en: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: With CAD models, every single trick I have tried to make photo mock-ups with an AI image-to-image conversion, whether using a line art or canny edge detector or just a shaded source object, has seen the AI ultimately ignore the cues in some generations, no matter what I do, and I would expect it to work a lot better with room photography.
sentences.zh: 对于 CAD 模型，我尝试通过 AI 图像到图像转换来制作照片模型的每一个技巧，无论是使用线条艺术、精明的边缘检测器还是仅使用阴影源对象，都看到 AI 最终忽略了某些世代的线索，无论我做什么，我希望它在室内摄影方面能更好地工作。

blocks.html_en:
In the 1960s Campbells Soup got in trouble with the FTC for using marbles to raise the ingredients and make the soup look fuller than it was. This was the real standard for deceptive advertising.

I dont care about simulating furniture placement specifically, but most use of AI in advertising that I see today would not be acceptable under that standard.

sentences.en: In the 1960s Campbells Soup got in trouble with the FTC for using marbles to raise the ingredients and make the soup look fuller than it was.
sentences.zh: 20 世纪 60 年代，金宝汤 (Campbells Soup) 因使用弹珠来提升原料并使汤看起来比实际更饱满而与联邦贸易委员会 (FTC) 发生纠纷。

sentences.en: This was the real standard for deceptive advertising.I dont care about simulating furniture placement specifically, but most use of AI in advertising that I see today would not be acceptable under that standard.

content_html_zh: 这太棒了！ 许多纽约人通过 StreetEasy 寻找公寓。 过去几年，人工智能公寓充斥其中。 AI 舞台会扭曲房间以适应 100% 肯定不适合的家具。 它具有欺骗性，我很高兴它至少现在需要披露（尽管我希望它被完全禁止）不会撒谎，我希望他们也将平方英尺作为法律要求。这让我完全困惑，但纽约是我居住过的美国唯一一个真正存在问题的主要城市。 在所有其他城市，我对此没有任何问题，几乎每个在线帖子都有平方英尺。与此同时，在 StreetEasy（以及列出纽约出租单位的其他平台）上，寻找公寓是一个主要的痛苦，因为大多数人的平方英尺信息为零。 然后它就变成了一个纯粹的猜谜游戏，变得超级烦人，因为我可能感兴趣的公寓只被列为“一间卧室”，但仅看照片无法判断它是 400 平方英尺还是 900 平方英尺。 知道这些信息会让租房者更容易，而且我想不出不提供这些信息的合理理由。纽约的许多房源上都有平方英尺，但这是错误的。 他们通常会列出公寓所占总面积的平方英尺，包括所有内墙和柱子，这些内墙和柱子可占据 20% 的面积。我们在美国（州一级）基本上也有这些法规，但我们只是不重复使用它们。 当然，关于什么是宜居、什么是不宜居是有规则的。 阳台则不然。 没有窗户的房间甚至不是卧室。 那种东西。然后将其转换为计算平方英尺确实是微不足道的，许多州就是这样做的。 但并非全部。 这是一个动机问题，不一定是能力或成本问题。回到第三世界，我记得我的父母查阅了该地区每平方米的参考价格，并将价格除以该价格来估算尺寸。广告上的金额会减少 20-25%。 当你向他们质问时，房地产经纪人只会给你真实的数字，我想大多数人都不会经历这个麻烦，所以对他们来说值得尝试。在某些国家/地区，就像海报中提到的那样，它们包括“建成”尺寸，而不是实际的宜居尺寸。 有时阳台也包含在其中，这是一种误导。 900 平方英尺的宜居面积中的两间卧室与 900 平方英尺的建筑面积（​​包括阳台）中的 3 间卧室是一回事。... 呵呵。 这实际上解释了我之前一直困惑的事情；该网站上的人声称 1000 平方英尺的公寓很小。 我想，如果你包括公寓的整个占地面积，包括阳台，那是肯定的。
